questions.subject: non_verbal
questions.body: How many cubes are there in the group?

questions.subject: non_verbal
questions.body: How many cubes are there in this figure?

solutions.solution: (b) There are only 12 edges in a cube/cuboid.
(d) All options are correct.
(d) A cube has 3 pair of opposite faces as it has 6 faces in total and each face has one opposite face.
(b) There are only 4 adjacent faces for each face of a cube.
(d) Hidden faces = 3
(d) A cube has 12 edges out of which 9 are visible ∴ Invisible edges = 12 - 9 = 3
(c) When a cube is seen from any direction, then at a time a maximum of only 7 corners are visible out of 8. Hence, only 1 corner is hidden.
(d) All of the above
(d) Required number of smaller cubes Volume of bigger cube / Volume of smaller cube = (3)³ / 1³ = (3)³ = 27
(b) Required number of times = (Side of bigger cube / Side of smaller cube) × 3 = (4/2 - 1) × 3 = (2 - 1) × 3 = 3
(d) In 24 small cubes two sides would be seen.

(c) Volume of bigger cube / Volume of smaller cube = Number of smaller cubes ⇒ 25 × 25 × 25 / Volume of smaller cube = 125 ⇒ Volume of smaller cube = 25 × 25 × 25 / 125 = 125 ∴ Side of smaller cube = ∛125 = 5 cm
(c) Required number of cuts = (Side of bigger cube / Side of smaller cube - 1) × 3 = (3/1 - 1) × 3 = 2 × 3 = 6
(c) Number of smaller cubes = Volume of bigger cube / Volume of smaller cube = (3)³ / (1)³ = (3)³ = 27
(c) Corner cubes are always 8.
(b) Number of middle cubes = 12(n - 2) = 12(3 - 2) = 12 × 1 = 12
(b) Number of central cubes = 6(n - 2)² = 6 × (3 - 2)² = 6 × 1 = 6
(a) Number of inner central cubes = (3 - 2)³ = (3 - 2)³ = 1
(c) Required parts = Side of bigger cube / Side of smaller cube = 3/1 = 3

(b) It is clear from the figure, that there are four blocks in the lower part close to the ground. Also, there are 3 blocks standing over the lower layer of blocks. Thus, there are 4 + 3 = 7 blocks in the given figure
(b) [Figure with numbered grid] So, there are 12 cubes in the given figure, which are illustrated above.

(c) From the given question figure, it is clear that 4 columns containing 1 cube each 1 column containing 3 cubes 4 columns containing 2 cubes each ∴ Total cubes = (4 × 1) + (1 × 3) + (4 × 2) = 4 + 3 + 8 = 15
